title: Buzzer-Beating 3 Sends Duke Past LSU 87-85 in Thrilling Sweet 16 Upset
body:
In a thrilling conclusion to their Sweet 16 matchup, No. 3 seed Duke edged out No. 2 seed LSU 87-85 on Friday night, courtesy of a breathtaking buzzer-beating three-pointer from Ashlon Jackson. The shot, which rolled around the rim before dropping through the net, sent the Blue Devils' bench into a frenzy and catapulted them into the Elite Eight.

Jackson's game-winner marked a pivotal moment in the women's NCAA Tournament, underscoring the importance of clutch performances in high-stakes competition. The freshman guard's ice-in-his-veins composure under pressure has been a hallmark of Duke's season, and Friday's heroics were the culmination of months of hard work and dedication. "It's an incredible feeling, knowing that I was able to help my team reach the next round," Jackson exclaimed in the post-game press conference. "We've been working towards this moment all season, and to have it pay off like this is just amazing."

Friday's contest was a back-and-forth affair, with both teams trading baskets and neither able to gain a clear advantage until the final seconds. LSU's talented roster, featuring a trio of double-digit scorers, pushed the Tigers to a 5-point lead with just over 2 minutes remaining. However, the Blue Devils refused to back down, fueled by Jackson's clutch shooting and a stifling defensive effort that forced multiple turnovers down the stretch. "Our team showed incredible heart and resilience tonight," Duke head coach said in the post-game press conference. "We knew it was going to be a battle, and our kids stepped up when it mattered most."

Duke's victory represents a testament to the program's continued growth and success under its current leadership. The Blue Devils have now won three consecutive NCAA Tournament games, with each victory coming in a different fashion. "We've been in tough situations before, but we've always found a way to come out on top," Jackson noted. "Tonight was no exception." With their Sweet 16 win, Duke has earned a spot in the women's Elite Eight, where they will face a stiff challenge from either Stanford or Maryland. "We're not done yet," the Duke coach emphasized. "We're just getting started."

As the tournament enters its final stages, fans and pundits alike will be eagerly watching the performances of teams like Duke, which has proven its mettle in the biggest games. With Jackson's clutch shooting and a roster full of talented players, the Blue Devils are poised for a deep run in the NCAA Tournament. "We believe in ourselves, and we know that we have the talent and the chemistry to make a deep run," Jackson said confidently. As the Blue Devils prepare to face their next opponent, one thing is clear: they are a force to be reckoned with.

The women's NCAA Tournament has been marked by its unpredictable nature, with upsets and close calls aplenty throughout the bracket. However, with the Sweet 16 now complete, one thing is certain: the road to the championship will be filled with formidable opponents and nail-biting finishes. As the remaining teams battle for a spot in the Final Four, fans can expect more thrilling performances and unforgettable moments. With Duke's victory, the stage is set for a thrilling conclusion to the tournament.
